info.title: VidJutsu API
info.description: VidJutsu is a video API built for agents to operate directly. Your agent can clone viral trending skits now: stage a trending clip with /v1/videos/download/tiktok or /v1/videos/download/instagram, check it for cloneability, generate a character and a clean starting frame, then render the clone. The pipeline runs end to end in about 5 minutes, measured. Kling motion control is the default video model; Seedance is the alternate. Clone video output is 480p 9:16.

tags.name: Cloning
tags.description: Your agent can Clone Viral Trending Skits Now. These endpoints check whether a staged source video is worth cloning, generate the character and starting frame, and render and poll the final clone video.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

get.summary: List accounts or get by ID
get.description: Returns a single account if ?id= is provided, or a list of all accounts. Supports tag filtering via ?tag.key=value.

201.description: The request has succeeded and a new resource has been created as a result.

get.summary: List assets or get by ID
get.description: Returns a single asset if ?id= is provided, or a list. Filter by ?type=video or ?tag.key=value.

post.description: Register a machine client per the auth.md protocol. Two identity types are supported: `anonymous` (returns an API key immediately with pre-claim scopes) and `verified_email` (kicks off OTP, returns only a claim_token). Both flows are user-claimed — the agent and user run the entire ceremony with no agent-provider participation required.
Initiate the email-verification claim flow for a previously-issued anonymous registration. Sends a 6-digit OTP to the supplied email and returns a claim_attempt_id.

Complete the email-verification claim flow by submitting the OTP. On success the registration's claimStatus flips to email_verified, post-claim scopes are unlocked, and a freshly-rotated API key is returned. The original pre-claim `vj_anon_*` key is invalidated server-side as a hygiene measure — agents MUST swap it for the new credential.
Revoke a previously-issued credential. Accepts a logout JWT body (application/logout+jwt) for future provider-driven revocation; currently a no-op receiver, returns 200.
Validates the 6-digit code and returns API credentials if correct. Code expires after 5 minutes.
Sends a 6-digit verification code to the email. Always returns sent: true to prevent email enumeration.

post.summary: Create a reusable, persisted character image
post.description: Generates a photoreal character identity image from a text prompt, optionally guided by one HTTPS reference image, and persists it durably under an id. Returns the character id plus a CDN-hosted image URL; reuse the id later via cloneStartingImage's characterId or fetch it again with GET /v1/characters/{id}. Synchronous; typically completes within a minute. Shares the daily clone admission limit.

403.description: HTTP 403 Forbidden — a valid API key with no active subscription called a gated endpoint. Body is the standard ApiError with error="subscription_required".

429.description: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests — a per-endpoint daily rate limit was exceeded. The limit window is fixed and resets at 00:00 UTC. Body is the standard ApiError; a Retry-After header carries the seconds until reset.

get.summary: Fetch a persisted character by id
get.description: Returns a single character owned by the caller. Unknown ids and other tenants' ids return 404. Status reads are not billed.

post.summary: Evaluate whether a source video can be cloned reliably
post.description: Runs a synchronous, deterministic cloneability analysis of a staged source video and returns a verdict (strong, possible, or weak), a 0-100 score, and the evidence behind them, so the agent can branch on the result, for example stopping on a weak verdict. The videoUrl must be public HTTPS and staged first: provider CDNs cannot fetch TikTok or Instagram links directly, so download the source with /v1/videos/download/tiktok or /v1/videos/download/instagram before checking it. The source video must be at most ~2 minutes long. Shares the daily clone admission limit.

403.description: HTTP 403 Forbidden — a valid API key with no active subscription called a gated endpoint. Body is the standard ApiError with error="subscription_required".

429.description: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests — a per-endpoint daily rate limit was exceeded. The limit window is fixed and resets at 00:00 UTC. Body is the standard ApiError; a Retry-After header carries the seconds until reset.

post.description: Composes a clean starting frame for a clone by applying the prompt to the character image, optionally grounded in the source video's first frame. Supply exactly one of characterImageUrl (a public HTTPS image) or characterId (a previously created character's id, from createCharacter or GET /v1/characters) to identify the character; supplying both or neither is a validation error. The returned image is guaranteed to be a clean cinematic frame with no text overlays, captions, subtitles, watermarks, or platform UI, regardless of what is present in the source video or requested prompt. All other URLs must be public HTTPS. Synchronous; typically completes within a minute. Shares the daily clone admission limit.

403.description: HTTP 403 Forbidden — a valid API key with no active subscription called a gated endpoint. Body is the standard ApiError with error="subscription_required".

429.description: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests — a per-endpoint daily rate limit was exceeded. The limit window is fixed and resets at 00:00 UTC. Body is the standard ApiError; a Retry-After header carries the seconds until reset.

post.summary: Clone source motion with Kling motion control or Seedance
post.description: Submits an asynchronous motion-clone generation. The default kling model uses Kling motion control to puppet the starting-image identity with the source video's motion, keeping the original sound; model="seedance" is the alternate model, rendering 9:16 at 480p via video-edit. All URLs must be public HTTPS. Source clips are capped at 15 seconds; a longer sourceVideoUrl is rejected with a 422 and an error of "video_too_long". Returns 202 with a task id to poll via GET /v1/clones/video/{id}.

202.description: The request has been accepted for processing, but processing has not yet completed.

get.description: Returns the current status of a clone video task owned by the caller. While status is "processing", poll again later; on "completed" the videoUrl field carries the HTTPS result URL, and on "failed" the error field explains why. Unknown ids and other tenants' ids return 404. Status reads are not billed.

code.description: 6-digit verification code emailed to the principal.

credential.description: Freshly-rotated API key. For anonymous registrations the original `vj_anon_*` key returned at signup is now invalid; agents MUST replace it with this value.

claim_token.description: Opaque claim_token returned from the initial /v1/auth/agent call.

email.description: Email address to send the verification code to.

expires_at.description: Unix epoch ms when this claim attempt expires.

requested_credential_type.description: Requested credential type. Currently only api_key is supported.

credential_type.description: Credential type returned, when issued immediately. Omitted on flows that require claim_token first.

credential.description: Live credential (API key) issued by the server, when one is issued immediately.

credential_expires.description: Unix epoch ms when the credential expires, or null/undefined for non-expiring keys.

scopes.description: Scopes the credential is authorized for at issue time.

post_claim_scopes.description: Scopes that will be added after the claim flow completes.

claim_url.description: URL the agent should direct the principal to in order to complete claim/verification.

claim_token.description: Opaque token used to complete the claim flow.

claim_token_expires.description: Unix epoch ms when the claim_token expires.

assertion.description: Email address the agent asserts on behalf of the principal.

imageUrl.description: HTTPS CDN URL of the character image.

model.description: Provider model id that produced the image.

rules.description: VidLang rules config. All rules off by default — enable explicitly. E.g. { "VL013": true, "VL003": false, "VL011": { "severity": "warning" } }

videoUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the source video to evaluate. http:// URLs are rejected.

context.description: Optional freeform context about the intended clone (product, character, constraints) to sharpen the analysis.

verdict.description: Overall cloneability verdict: "strong" clones reliably, "possible" may need retries or manual QA, "weak" is unlikely to clone well.

evidence.description: Concrete observations from the video that support the verdict.

model.description: Provider model id that produced the analysis.

characterImageUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the character identity image. http:// URLs are rejected. Exactly one of characterImageUrl or characterId must be supplied.

characterId.description: Id of a previously created character (from createCharacter or GET /v1/characters) whose stored image should be used. Exactly one of characterImageUrl or characterId must be supplied.

prompt.description: Instructions for composing the starting frame (pose, framing, scene).

sourceVideoUrl.description: Optional public HTTPS URL of the source video whose opening frame should ground the composition.

id.description: Clone task id. Poll GET /v1/clones/video/{id} with it.

startingImageUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the starting frame that carries the target identity. http:// URLs are rejected.

sourceVideoUrl.description: Public HTTPS URL of the source video whose motion is cloned. http:// URLs are rejected.

model.description: Generation model. Defaults to "kling": Kling motion control puppets the character image with the source video's motion and keeps the original sound. "seedance" is the alternate model (9:16 at 480p, video-edit).

prompt.description: Optional override for the default identity-swap prompt (seedance only).

videoUrl.description: HTTPS URL of the finished clone. Present only when status is "completed".

error.description: Failure explanation. Present only when status is "failed".

riskScore.description: Composite risk score 0-100 computed deterministically from violation severities.

policySnapshotDate.description: ISO date of the newest active policy snapshot this scan was evaluated against.

disclaimer.description: Informational disclaimer. This response is not legal advice.

text.description: Exact quoted clause from the platform's policy page.

sourceUrl.description: URL to the source policy page (may include #anchor).

policyVersion.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the policy snapshot this citation was drawn from.

timestamp.description: Timestamp (seconds) in the source video where the evidence appears.

visualDescription.description: Short description of the visual evidence, if any.

text.description: The text to evaluate — caption, ad copy, draft script, upload description, etc.

kind.description: Optional label for logs; does not affect rule selection.

context.description: Optional publication metadata that affects risk (caption, hashtags, monetization intent).

videoUrl.description: Public URL of the video to scan.

format.description: Optional content format hint. Inferred from duration when omitted.

context.description: Optional publication metadata that affects risk (caption, hashtags, monetization intent).

ruleId.description: Stable identifier of the matched rule in our compliance corpus.

category.description: Rule category, e.g. monetization, community-guidelines, ad-policy, copyright, minors.

explanation.description: Plain-language reasoning tying the video to the cited rule. Informational, not legal advice.

prompt.description: Text description of the character to generate.

referenceImageUrl.description: Optional public HTTPS URL of a reference image to guide identity. http:// URLs are rejected.

id.description: Persisted character id (char_...). Reuse it later via cloneStartingImage's characterId or GET /v1/characters/{id}.

imageUrl.description: HTTPS CDN URL of the generated character image.

model.description: Provider model id that produced the image.

DailyLimits.description: Per-endpoint daily request limits, keyed by endpoint name (fixed window, resets 00:00 UTC).

text.description: Disclaimer text. Use \\n for line breaks. Kept fine-print style at the bottom of the frame.

fontSize.description: Font size in pixels. Defaults to 2.2% of video height (fine-print).

resultUrl.description: CDN URL of the disclaimed video

project.description:
The serializable timeline document (Twick ProjectJSON: tracks, assets,
metadata, version). Stored opaquely; the editor owns its shape.

mediaUrl.description: URL of the video to extract from

frames.description: Frame indices to extract. Use [0, 75, 150] for specific frames, 'auto' for 3 evenly spaced, or 'last' for the final frame

metadata.description: Return video metadata (width, height, fps, duration). Defaults to true

imageUrl.description: HTTPS CDN URL of the generated image.

model.description: Provider model id that produced the image.

dailyLimits.description: Per-endpoint daily request limits (fixed window, resets 00:00 UTC).

characters.description: Characters owned by the caller, most recent first.

text.description: Overlay text. Use \\n for line breaks.

fontSize.description: Font size in pixels. Defaults to 4% of video height.

resultUrl.description: CDN URL of the overlaid video

dailyLimits.description: Per-endpoint daily request limits (fixed window, resets 00:00 UTC).

data.description: Pass-through response from Scrape Creators. Shape varies per endpoint. Media URLs are the raw provider source URLs; scrape never stages media. To stage a specific video's file, call the single-video download endpoint.

trim.description: Reduce response size by trimming large fields

required.description: Whether an active subscription is required for gated endpoints.

mediaUrl.description: URL of the video or audio to transcribe
URL of the media to analyze

prompt.description: Freeform prompt — tell AI what to look for

response.description: Structured JSON response from AI based on the prompt
